name: Portuense Portuguese

allophone_rules.notes: Northern betacism: the /v/ ~ /b/ phonological opposition is lost and both merge into a single phoneme /b/, realised [b] in all positions (vinho -> [ˈbiɲu], vaca -> [ˈbakɐ], estava -> [ʃˈtabɐ]). This is the primary trait separating Northern from Central-Southern European Portuguese. Cintra (1971; Boletim de Filologia 22:81-116): 'o desaparecimento da oposição fonológica entre os fonemas /v/ e /b/ e a sua fusão num fonema único /b/'; corroborated by Segura (2013). Modelled as a post-lexical phoneme-side rewrite (the phoneme /v/, produced by the inherited pt-PT grapheme map, is realised [b]) rather than a grapheme substitution, so the merger applies uniformly however /v/ arises.

Diphthongisation of the tonic CLOSE-mid front vowel: stressed close [e] -> rising diphthong [je] (mês -> [ˈmjeʃ], ele -> [ˈjelɨ]). Cintra (1971) isolates this as the defining, 'tão caracterizadora' feature of the Baixo-Minho / Douro-Litoral (Porto) area: 'a ditongação, tão caracterizadora, das vogais tónicas fechadas [e] em [je], [o] em [wo] (por vezes [wɔ])'; corroborated by Segura (2013), who names diphthongisation of stressed vowels the characteristic trait of the north-western variety. Gated to the CLOSE [e] only: genuinely open tonic [ɛ] (pé, café) does NOT diphthongise. LIMIT: the inherited pt-PT grapheme map selects the OPEN allophone for spelling-unmarked stressed <e> (open/close is lexical, not predictable from orthography), so words whose standard value is close [e] but which the base engine transcribes [ɛ] (e.g. cedo, medo) are not reached by this rule; forcing them would wrongly diphthongise true open vowels. The rule fires wherever the engine does yield close [e] in a stressed nucleus (mês, ele, este, pêlo).

Diphthongisation of the tonic CLOSE-mid back vowel: stressed close [o] -> rising diphthong [wo] (por vezes [wɔ]) (avô -> [ɐˈbwo], pôr -> [ˈpwoɾ], ovo -> [ˈwobu]; the emblematic Porto -> [ˈpwoɾtu]). Same Cintra (1971) passage as PT_PORTO_DIPHTHONGISE_E; Segura (2013). Gated to CLOSE [o]; genuinely open tonic [ɔ] (avó, só) does not diphthongise. Same open/close pre-lexical LIMIT as the /e/ rule: 'Porto' has an underlying close /o/ but the base engine mis-selects open [ɔ], so the emblematic [ˈpwoɾtu] is only produced once the pre-lexical vowel is close; the rule is deliberately not widened to [ɔ] because that would diphthongise true open vowels (café, avó, só).

notes: Portuense / Northern European Portuguese of Porto, Greater Porto and the Baixo-Minho / Douro-Litoral zone (Cintra's 'Baixo-Minho e Douro-Litoral'), one of the most idiosyncratic EP varieties (Cintra 1971; Segura 2013). Deltas over standard (Lisbon-type) pt-PT: (1) BETACISM — the /v/~/b/ opposition is lost, both merging in /b/ (allophone rule PT_PORTO_BETACISM; Cintra feature 1); (2) tonic-CLOSED-vowel DIPHTHONGISATION — stressed close [e]->[je], close [o]->[wo]/[wɔ] (allophone rules PT_PORTO_DIPHTHONGISE_E/_O), Cintra's single defining Porto marker; (3) DIPHTHONG PRESERVATION — <ou>->[ow] and <ei>->[ej] kept where Lisbon monophthongises/lowers ([o], [ɐj]) (inherited-map grapheme deltas; Cintra feature 4). All other EP processes (unstressed vowel reduction, dark coda /l/, coda-sibilant chiado, the coda-s sandhi) are inherited unchanged from pt-PT. KNOWN LIMITS (documented, not faked): (a) Cintra's primary North/South isogloss — the apico-alveolar sibilant [s̺ z̺] vs the standard predorsodental [s̪ z̪] — is an articulatory place distinction invisible to a phoneme-level, orthography-blind engine (Cintra 1971 feature 2; it is his most diagnostic feature yet the least simulable); it is left as a surface variant in `allophones` (ʃ->[ʃ,ʂ], ʒ->[ʒ,ʐ]) for documentation only and is NOT asserted as the default realisation. (b) The diphthongisation rules are gated to the CLOSE mid vowels [e]/[o]; because open/close for spelling-unmarked stressed <e>/<o> is lexical and the inherited pt-PT map defaults to the OPEN allophone, some words with an underlying close vowel (cedo, medo, Porto) are transcribed with open [ɛ]/[ɔ] pre-lexically and therefore escape diphthongisation — widening the rule to the open vowels would wrongly diphthongise genuinely open tonic vowels (café, avó, só) and is rejected. BENCHMARK HONESTY: the ep_dialects `pt-PT-x-north` expert gold was annotated for betacism and diphthong preservation but NOT for tonic-vowel diphthongisation, so PT_PORTO_DIPHTHONGISE_E/_O raise PER slightly against that (incomplete) expert gold while being the correctly-sourced defining Porto feature; they are kept per the honesty gate (B8 precedent: a source-correct rule that only regresses a gold lacking the annotation still ships, with the divergence documented). Sub-lexical/sporadic Porto features (l-palatalisation quilo->quilho, Filipa->[fʎipɐ]) are lexical and not modelled. NATIVE-SPEAKER VALIDATION (Portuguese With Leo, 'Sotaque e expressões do Porto', https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kOxLQR8M3TQ): the Porto speaker Bárbara confirms the modelled betacism ('no Porto trocam-se sempre os Vs pelos Bs ... baca [vaca]', 'coisa que eu habitualmente troco') and the more open vowels ('o sotaque do Porto é mais aberto do que o de Lisboa', 'abéarta'); the prothetic glide 'ieu' for eu / '-i antes das vogais' / '-a final' ('ieua') is a lexical/prosodic trait left unmodelled. The Trás-os-Montes deep-dive (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=70a_vNEa_aw) corroborates that <ou>->[ow] and open vowels are Porto/north-western ('eles abrem mais as vogais') traits.

ancestors.notes: Standard (Lisbon-type) European Portuguese; Porto diverges by betacism (v->b), tonic-closed-vowel diphthongisation, and diphthong preservation (ei/ou).

sources.author: Cintra, Luís F. Lindley
sources.title: Nova proposta de classificação dos dialectos galego-portugueses
sources.publisher: Boletim de Filologia (Centro de Estudos Filológicos, Lisboa), vol. 22, pp. 81-116
sources.notes: Foundational classification of the Galician-Portuguese dialect space by maximally diagnostic phonetic features. Feature 1 (betacism): 'o desaparecimento da oposição fonológica entre os fonemas /v/ e /b/ e a sua fusão num fonema único /b/'. Defining Porto/Baixo-Minho/Douro-Litoral feature: 'a ditongação, tão caracterizadora, das vogais tónicas fechadas [e] em [je], [o] em [wo] (por vezes [wɔ])'. Feature 2 (the apico-alveolar sibilant), his primary North/South isogloss, is an articulatory distinction not simulable at the phoneme/orthography level — documented as a spec limit, not modelled. Republished in Cintra, Estudos de Dialectologia Portuguesa, Sá da Costa, 1983, pp. 117-163.

sources.author: Segura, Luísa
sources.title: Variedades dialetais do português europeu
sources.publisher: In Raposo, E.B.P. et al. (eds.), Gramática do Português, vol. I, Fundação Calouste Gulbenkian, Lisboa, pp. 85-142
sources.notes: Modern EP dialectology synthesis. Names diphthongisation of stressed vowels as the characteristic trait of the north-western (Baixo-Minho / Douro-Litoral, Porto) variety and describes it as one of the most idiosyncratic EP varieties; documents Northern betacism. Corroborating second source for the Cintra features modelled here.

sources.author: Mateus, M.H.M. & d'Andrade, E.
sources.title: The Phonology of Portuguese
sources.notes: Reference EP phonology for the inherited base processes (unstressed vowel reduction, dark coda /l/, coda sibilants) that Porto inherits unchanged from pt-PT.

sources.author: Portuguese With Leo (with Bárbara, portuense)
sources.title: Sotaque e expressões do Porto
sources.notes: Native-speaker Porto deep-dive (NOT academic). Validates the modelled betacism ('no Porto trocam-se sempre os Vs pelos Bs', 'baca' for vaca) and open vowels ('mais aberto do que Lisboa', 'abéarta'); notes the unmodelled prothetic 'ieu'/'ieua' (i-onglide + final -a) as a lexical/prosodic Porto shibboleth.

stress.notes: Acordo Ortográfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam).
